skill.name: skill-standardization
skill.description: Standardize and validate SKILL.md files against the Agent Skills specification. Use it to create compliant skills, audit frontmatter, improve trigger descriptions, and add evaluation cases.
skill.summary: Validate, standardize, and improve Agent Skills files for reliable agent use.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the high weak-cryptography alerts as false positives caused by normal skill documentation, examples, and file names. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill includes and recommends a Bash validation script that runs local parsing commands on user-selected skill directories. No evidence found of malicious intent, data exfiltration, destructive behavior, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Local Bash Validator Executes Shell Utilities
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run scripts/validate_skill.sh, and that script uses grep, sed, awk, xargs, basename, globbing, and arithmetic command substitutions. This is legitimate validation behavior, but it expands the runtime surface because user-selected paths are processed by a shell script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static command-execution findings are confirmed in context, but the commands are normal local validation utilities with quoted variables. No network transfer or destructive command was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts point to documentation text, examples, SKILL.md naming, and evaluation descriptions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic operations being used for security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed the flagged files and found skill metadata, documentation examples, and evaluation assertions rather than cryptographic code. The script contains no crypto operations.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Do Not Perform Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are reference links to agentskills.io documentation. They are not fetched by code and do not send local data anywhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the References section. No code path was found that opens these links or transmits information to them.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Validation Checks
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary checks for valid names, directories, file existence, and example validation output. No evidence found of host fingerprinting, credential discovery, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are part of local SKILL.md validation and documentation examples. They do not inspect sensitive system state beyond user-provided skill paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Script Execution Recommended By Skill
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends running a bundled Bash script against local skill directories. This pattern should be disclosed because marketplace users may not expect a documentation skill to invoke shell tooling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit in the skill instructions and script shebang. The script purpose is benign, but shell execution remains a meaningful capability.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need consistent SKILL.md files that trigger reliably and pass format checks. This skill validates structure, improves descriptions, and helps create compliant evaluation cases.
actual_capabilities: Validates required SKILL.md frontmatter fields and recommended sections.
Checks skill names for lowercase hyphenated format and directory matching.
Reviews description length and trigger phrasing for better activation.
Creates a standard SKILL.md outline with frontmatter, instructions, examples, and references.
Helps convert legacy headings into the documented Agent Skills structure.
Guides creation of evaluation prompts and verifiable assertions.
limitations: It validates formatting conventions, not the runtime safety of every skill implementation.
The bundled script uses simple shell parsing and may miss complex YAML cases.
It depends on local filesystem access to read skill directories.
It references the Agent Skills specification but does not fetch updates automatically.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New Skill for Review
use_cases.description: Create a compliant SKILL.md with clear triggers, standard sections, and evaluation ideas before publishing.

use_cases.title: Audit an Internal Skill Library
use_cases.description: Run batch validation across many skill folders and identify missing sections or naming problems.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Activation Quality
use_cases.description: Rewrite vague descriptions into specific trigger instructions that work better in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

prompt_templates.title: Validate One Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for one local skill folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the SKILL.md file in this skill directory and list any errors, warnings, and recommended fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Standard Skill File
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a new skill from a short idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standard SKILL.md for a skill named [skill-name]. It should handle [tasks] and include trigger-focused description text.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Weak Description
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimize trigger behavior for an existing skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill description and rewrite it so the skill triggers reliably for relevant user intents while staying under 1024 characters.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Audit and Prioritize Fixes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for a folder of skills.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate every skill under this directory, group issues by severity, and recommend the smallest set of edits needed for marketplace readiness.

output_examples.input: Validate a skill folder with a missing Examples section and a vague description.
output: Reports that required frontmatter is present.
Warns that the description lacks clear trigger language.
Warns that the Examples section is missing.
Suggests a more specific description and section outline.

output_examples.input: Create a new git-workflow skill from a short request.
output: Produces a SKILL.md outline with standard frontmatter.
Includes clear usage scenarios for commits, branches, and pull requests.
Adds examples and best practices suitable for agent execution.

output_examples.input: Improve a short description that only says the skill helps with CSV files.
output: Rewrites the description with concrete CSV operations.
Adds trigger keywords such as import, clean, transform, and summarize.
Keeps the result concise enough for the specification limit.

best_practices: Run validation before publishing and after major edits to SKILL.md.
Keep descriptions specific, trigger-focused, and under the specification limit.
Use verifiable evaluation assertions that can be checked by a reviewer or test harness.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague descriptions such as helps with files without concrete operations.
Do not place long reference material directly in SKILL.md when a references folder fits better.
Do not treat validator success as a complete security review of executable skill code.

faq.question: Does this skill create complete Agent Skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft a standard SKILL.md structure with frontmatter, instructions, examples, best practices, and references.

faq.question: Can it validate many skills at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundled validator supports a batch mode for directories that contain multiple skill folders.

faq.question: Does it require shell access?
faq.answer: Shell access is needed for the bundled validation script. Manual review of SKILL.md content can still be done without running it.

faq.question: Does it check security risks in skill code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Agent Skills format, descriptions, sections, and evaluation structure. Security review must be separate.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What makes a good skill description?
faq.answer: A good description states specific operations, clear trigger conditions, related keywords, and important edge cases.
